metrics.text: Revenue ~$12M GMV (internal estimate — Tony has not confirmed actuals). 300–400 orders/day including quotes and repair authorizations; 60% through website, 40% via phone/manual PO. 65–70% straight price book purchases, 30–35% custom quoting. 40,000 SKU-intensive catalog — market leader in fitness equipment parts space. Previous failed replatform cost $250K+. Soft launch target: EOY 2026. Full relaunch: early 2027.

economic_buyer.text: Vince Smith (Interim CEO / Chairman, PE firm) is the real economic buyer — has empowered Tony to 'just go' with no lengthy approval process. Tony Meirovitz (VP E-commerce, 4 weeks tenure) is DM for day-to-day decisions; oversees E-commerce, Marketing, and Customer Service. Mike Montoy (Head of Operations, 22+ years) is key operational stakeholder. All three attended May 20 discovery call.

decision_criteria.text: B2B + D2C on Shopify Plus. Jordan confirmed 80% OOB Shopify (meta fields + meta objects); 20% custom with DYODE. Custom modules: cable CPQ (fixed labor + component retail cost), electronics exchange and buyback program (separate pricing tier), repair return shipping labels (UPS API). Four-tier B2B pricing with volume discounts. Net 30 terms standard; credit card payments primary. Consolidated single shipping charge from two warehouses (LA electronics + Illinois general parts). Shopify native search first — upgrade to Algolia later only if needed (Tony has SearchSpring scar tissue). Flat file ERP bridge until NetSuite migration (before April 2027). AI/agentic commerce via Universal Commerce Protocol (auto-on with Shopify). AutoZone-style make/model/brand → parts filter with PDF line drawings and installation videos.

decision_process.text: DYODE (Tim Katz co-founder, Elana K. Director Ops & Product, Paul Sr. Dev/SA, Kevin Stites Director Web Dev) is confirmed implementation partner. May 20 discovery completed — Tony, Mike Montoy, Vince Smith attended. Next: Jordan + Mercedes reconnect with DYODE (Tim/Elana) to plan broader team demo. Mercedes proposed parallel contract review on call; Tony confirmed Vince moves fast with no lengthy approvals. Formal procurement path not yet mapped.

identified_pain.text: Legacy ERP (~20 years old) creates extreme site lag — 700+ individual API calls per part lookup. Current site not mobile-friendly (Google SEO penalties). Previous $250K replatform failed: not vetted, not tested, slammed to production — customers reverted immediately. Large B2B customers (24 Hour Fitness, LA Fitness) send 75–100 line item POs that CS agents manually key. Two warehouse locations cause double shipping charges and inability to mix cart items from both. Old failed site still being indexed by Google (Tony actioning).

champion.text: DYODE team (Tim Katz, Elana K., Paul SA, Kevin Stites) are strongest advocates — Tim: 'only group I would trust.' Tony Meirovitz (VP E-comm) is DM — 20+ years e-commerce experience, prior Shopify familiarity, DYODE ally. Vince Smith (Interim CEO/Chairman, PE) is executive sponsor who moves fast. Jordan Murphy (SE) confirmed 80% OOB Shopify fit on May 20 call.

competition.text: No direct competitors mentioned. Previous platform was a bespoke custom .NET site. Evaluating Shopify vs status quo rebuild. BigCommerce not raised. Tony has prior Shopify experience.

national-gym-supply.strategy: Discovery complete — Tony confirmed Shopify direction, Vince (PE/Interim CEO) is economic buyer with mandate to move fast. DYODE (Paul + Kevin) are the confirmed build team. Jordan confirmed 80% OOB Shopify on May 20. Critical path: (1) schedule DYODE reconnect + broader team demo, (2) send contracts for parallel review (Vince = fast approvals), (3) scope custom modules (cable CPQ, electronics exchange) and identify app vs custom route. Timeline: EOY 2026 soft launch → early 2027 full relaunch — contract must close Q3 2026.

risks.text: Custom module scope — cable CPQ, electronics exchange/buyback, repair return labels. Tony 'a little nervous' about customization. Must confirm app vs custom route with DYODE.
risks.mit: DYODE (Paul SA) to assess at next reconnect. Identify Shopify app options for CPQ and exchange programs before scoping custom dev.

risks.text: Revenue/GMV not confirmed on May 20 call — Tony hasn't shared actuals. $12M is internal estimate. Affects Shopify Plus pricing conversation.
risks.mit: Ask Tony directly for 2025 revenue numbers on DYODE reconnect call.

risks.text: Jul 15 close date — soft launch EOY 2026 requires Q3 contract; Vince moves fast but DYODE quote turnaround time unknown.
risks.mit: Align on timeline at DYODE reconnect. Confirm July build start is achievable given 5-6 month build.

risks.text: Search is Tony's top priority — SearchSpring scar tissue means bar is high. Shopify native search must be validated before commit.
risks.mit: Demo Shopify search and discovery on broader team call. Confirm serial number and part number fuzzy matching meets requirements.

done: Requirements doc received via DYODE — full custom module requirements documented
Key stakeholders identified: Tony Meirovitz (VP E-comm/DM), Tim Katz (DYODE), Elana K. (DYODE)

Contracts: Mercedes proposed parallel review on May 20 call — Tony confirmed Vince (PE/Interim CEO) moves fast with no lengthy approvals

national-gym-supply.notes: Introduced via DYODE partner (Tim Katz). Legacy ERP EOL + $250K failed replatform are the forcing functions. 300-400 orders/day, ~$12M GMV est., 40K SKUs. 'AutoZone for fitness equipment parts' — make/model/brand → parts filter. Two warehouses: LA (electronics repair), Illinois (general parts). Key attendees May 20: Tony Meirovitz (VP E-comm, tmeirovitz@gympart.com), Mike Montoy (Head of Ops, mmontoy@gympart.com), Vince Smith (Interim CEO/Chairman, vsmith@gympart.com). DYODE: Tim Katz (tim@dyode.com), Elana K., Paul (paul@dyode.com), Kevin Stites (kevin.stites@dyode.com).

manager_q.compelling_event: Legacy ERP EOL + $250K failed replatform. EOY 2026 soft launch target.
manager_q.urgency: EOY 2026 soft launch — Q3 contract required. Vince empowered to move fast.
manager_q.risk_to_close: Custom module scope unknown; contracts not yet sent

metrics.text: DTC online revenue ~$500K (excl. ~$1.5M acquisition brand just added). B2B online only ~$50K last year — vast majority of B2B done offline through reps/fax/email. Cost comparison: NetSuite SuiteCommerce = 3x current Magento spend. Total wholesale revenue unknown (Alli does not own that P&L).

economic_buyer.text: Not yet identified above Alli Boggs. Alli (Director of E-commerce) is primary contact and evaluator but flagged cost as a constraint she cannot unilaterally approve — final sign-off is above her. Buying committee not yet mapped.

decision_criteria.text: Must be cheaper than NetSuite SuiteCommerce (~3x Magento). Requires: NetSuite ERP feed for custom pricing + case pack configs, no-code site updates (Sidekick/AI), sales rep portal access to customer orders and tracking. Plus vs Advanced still an open question given DTC revenue size ($500K).

decision_process.text: Mercedes to send Plus vs Advanced comparison materials by Mon May 11. Alli to review. Technical follow-up scheduled after Allen DTC site #2 launches (~May 12–13). Broader buying committee and paper process not yet mapped.

identified_pain.text: Adobe Commerce (Magento) requires dev/IT consultant for simple site changes (banners, video). Two separate Magento instances (DTC + B2B) = operational overhead. B2B portal adoption near zero — reps still use fax/email/CS manually. Evaluating NetSuite SuiteCommerce as B2B replacement but it is 3x cost and Alli is not excited about it.

champion.text: Alli Boggs (Director of E-commerce). Engaged and enthusiastic about Shopify, driving the evaluation. Low financial authority — final say likely above her. Julie Marcott (AE buddy) co-owns relationship and joined May 7 call.

allen-company.strategy: DTC migration to Shopify Advanced is already happening across 4–5 brands. The strategic prize is native B2B on Plus — winning this before Alli gets locked into NetSuite SuiteCommerce. Competitive window: Alli explicitly said she is not excited about NetSuite for B2B. Priority: Plus vs Advanced comparison by Mon May 11, then technical call with SE to close the loop on case packs + ERP feed objections.

risks.text: Economic buyer not identified — Alli may not have budget authority for Plus
risks.mit: Identify EB on next technical call. Frame Plus ROI around B2B growth and dealer adoption to get exec attention.

risks.text: DTC revenue ($500K) may not justify Plus pricing — Advanced may be ceiling
risks.mit: Model Plus pricing relative to B2B growth potential. If Plus requires >$30K/year, deal may stay on Advanced.

risks.text: B2B timeline undefined — DTC migration could push B2B decision to Q4+
risks.mit: Send materials by Mon May 11, schedule technical call proactively. Don't let B2B become a 2027 conversation.

risks.text: NetSuite SuiteCommerce still on table as B2B option
risks.mit: Price delta (3x) is our best defense. Keep SuiteCommerce comparison explicit in materials.

done: Discovery call May 7 — Adobe Commerce pain documented, B2B requirements surfaced
Confirmed Shopify Payments eligibility (supportability doc complete, cleared May 5)
Confirmed Shopify supports: custom pricing per account, case pack configs, NetSuite ERP feed, sales rep portal
Identified competition: NetSuite SuiteCommerce (3x cost, low enthusiasm from Alli)
left: Send Plus vs Advanced comparison materials by Mon May 11 (action item from call)

Identify economic buyer above Alli Boggs

allen-company.notes: Director of E-commerce Alli Boggs (allison.boggs@allencompany.net) is primary contact. DTC already migrating to Shopify Advanced across 4–5 brands. B2B is the strategic play — currently $50K online but large offline dealer network (gun shops, ranges) not digitized. ERP: NetSuite. DTC off Magento by mid-to-late June. Competing with NetSuite SuiteCommerce which Alli finds uninspiring and 3x cost. Shopify Payments cleared May 5.

metrics.text: Revenue ~$35M, ~200 employees, London HQ. 90% of revenue is wholesale — almost entirely undigitized. ROI hypothesis: digitizing wholesale ordering could significantly reduce manual CS overhead and improve buyer experience for international accounts. No metrics quantified yet.

economic_buyer.text: Not yet identified. Nicole Freydell is primary contact (role/seniority unclear). No executive sponsor or budget owner mapped.

decision_criteria.text: Not yet fully surfaced. Known needs: B2B portal for specialty coffee wholesale buyers, integration requirements TBD (IT Director joining next call), multi-currency likely required (London HQ, international accounts).

decision_process.text: Transferred from Grant Dugas (SDR, who declined intro meeting). Intro call Apr 29 with Nicole. Jordan Murphy (SE) joined the deal and team is on Microsoft Teams. Technical deep dive May 26 — IT Director joining. Formal eval criteria, competing platforms, and wider stakeholders all unknown.

identified_pain.text: 90% of business is wholesale and almost none of it is digitized — customers ordering manually. WooCommerce DTC site not meeting B2B needs. Nicole confirmed desire for a B2B wholesale portal to replace manual processes.

champion.text: Nicole Freydell is engaged but title, seniority, and internal influence are unknown. No confirmed champion with budget authority.

caravela-coffee.strategy: THIN — 2/16 MEDDPICC. This deal needs discovery depth before it can advance. Priority on May 26 call: surface economic buyer and IT Director's authority, quantify the wholesale order volume pain (how many orders/day, how much manual work), and identify competing platforms being evaluated. The 90% wholesale angle is a perfect B2B story — but it needs a champion with authority.

risks.text: THIN QUALIFICATION: EB, competition, and decision criteria all unknown
risks.mit: May 26 call must surface EB and IT Director's role. Treat as a discovery call, not a demo.

risks.text: Transferred deal — Nicole's context and motivation are unclear
risks.mit: Understand why Grant declined the intro. Ensure Nicole has real authority to drive a decision.

risks.text: International HQ (London) may introduce VAT/compliance and multi-currency complexity
risks.mit: Confirm region and currency requirements before proposing solution.

risks.text: No stated internal deadline or project trigger
risks.mit: Surface urgency on next call — what's forcing action now?

done: Intro call Apr 29 — confirmed B2B wholesale need, platform is WooCommerce (DTC)
Jordan Murphy (SE) joined the deal

caravela-coffee.notes: Specialty coffee B2B wholesale, 90% wholesale, $35M rev, ~200 employees, London HQ. Contact: Nicole Freydell (nicole.freydell@caravela.coffee). Transferred from Grant Dugas (SDR). Current platform: WooCommerce (DTC only). SE Jordan Murphy joined. IT Director joining May 26 call on Microsoft Teams — critical discovery session. Confirm multi-currency, VAT, and ERP context.

metrics.text: PBR $165K. Business revenue, order volume, and SKU count not yet captured in discovery. ROI case not yet built — integration replacement (Salesforce + Sage) is the stated driver.

economic_buyer.text: Not yet identified. Kyle Boyd (kyle@artificialturfsupply.com) is primary contact but title and budget authority unknown. No executive sponsor mapped.

decision_criteria.text: Q4 go-live target (Oct 2026). Build starts July. Must integrate with Salesforce (CRM) and Sage (ERP/accounting) to replace legacy systems. Specific integration data flows not yet documented.

decision_process.text: Technical discovery completed May 5 with Kyle Boyd and Jordan Murphy (SE). Next meeting first week of July after build scope is confirmed. Formal eval process, competitive shortlist, and other stakeholders not yet mapped.

identified_pain.text: Legacy Salesforce + Sage integrations require replacement — current setup creating operational friction. Q4 switchover is a stated business target with build starting July, indicating real urgency to decide quickly.

champion.text: Kyle Boyd is engaged and actively moving the deal forward with Jordan Murphy (SE). Authority level not confirmed. Jordan Murphy is engaged as SE champion on the Shopify side.

artificial-turf-supply.strategy: THIN — 4/16 MEDDPICC. SE Jordan Murphy is the key asset here — leverage the July meeting to surface economic buyer, build the ROI case, and nail down integration scope for Salesforce + Sage before the July build start. The July gap (now to July) is 6+ weeks dark — stay in touch with Kyle to maintain momentum.

risks.text: THIN QUALIFICATION: EB not identified, metrics not captured
risks.mit: July meeting must surface EB and business case. Prep Jordan with EB discovery questions.

risks.text: 6-week gap May → July — deal can go cold
risks.mit: Monthly check-in with Kyle. Send value-add content (integration docs, case studies) to stay top of mind.

risks.text: Salesforce + Sage integration complexity could extend timeline past Q4
risks.mit: SE Jordan Murphy to map integration scope in July session. Identify certified SI partner early.

risks.text: No competitive intel — unknown what alternatives are being evaluated
risks.mit: Ask Kyle directly on next check-in: who else are you talking to?

Q4 switchover target and July build start confirmed

artificial-turf-supply.notes: Contact: Kyle Boyd (kyle@artificialturfsupply.com). SE: Jordan Murphy (jordan.murphy@shopify.com). Technical discovery May 5. Build starts July, Q4 go-live target. Integrations: Salesforce (CRM) + Sage (ERP/accounting) replacing legacy. Next meeting 1st week of July.

metrics.text: ~$5M B2B GMV potential (internal estimate). 137 dealers, 5 territories, 7 sales reps. 4 brands: Viaggio Pontoons, Lakota Trailers, SMC, Duo Form (Bristol, Indiana). Revenue not yet confirmed — Nathaniel to provide 2025 B2B/DTC split. Current platform cost: NetSuite SuiteCommerce ~$35K/yr. Quote process: dealer selects options → submits → sales reps recreate in NetSuite (no deposit, fully manual). Target go-live August 2027 (model year 2028 launch).

economic_buyer.text: Owner Eric (last name unknown) is likely final decision maker — attended Nathaniel's May 12 leadership pitch alongside CFO/Corporate Controller, Head of Sales, and Head of CS. All four stakeholders are now aware of the Shopify evaluation. Nathaniel (Director of IT) is day-to-day champion but lacks budget authority. Neither Mercedes nor Joe have direct contact with Eric or CFO yet.

decision_criteria.text: Unified Shopify B2B (dealer portal) + D2C (boat owner portal) on one platform. Dealer configurator with rule-based pricing logic. Multi-brand architecture must extend from Viaggio design to Lakota Trailers, SMC, Duo Form. Partner requirements: current tech standards, no IP lock-in, no AI buzzwords. Replace NetSuite SuiteCommerce. Proforma invoices for dealer financing. Two invoices per boat (boat + motor separately).

decision_process.text: Nathaniel pitched leadership week of May 12 — Owner, CFO, Head of Sales, Head of CS all attended. May 20 debrief — Mercedes' first call as B2B specialist. 8-page requirements doc arriving May 22-23. Technical discovery scheduled May 28-29 noon-1pm ET. Decision deadline: end of June 2026 — Nathaniel: 'if we don't have a decision by the end of June, we're going to be scrambling.' Co-sell: Joe Hemingway is AE (SF opp owner), Mercedes is B2B specialist SE.

identified_pain.text: NetSuite SuiteCommerce ~$35K/yr explicitly not built for B2B (confirmed by NetSuite engineers themselves). Simple SuiteCommerce changes take 6 months via third-party devs. Nathaniel is a team of one. Quote workflow is fully manual — dealer selects options, submits, then sales reps recreate the entire order in NetSuite with no deposit. Two separate invoices per boat (boat + motor) creates friction. Proforma invoices required for dealer financing add complexity.

champion.text: Nathaniel Talbot (Director of IT, newly promoted) is the primary internal champion — built the 5-year cost analysis and pitched leadership internally himself. Joe Hemingway (AE) brought the deal. Mercedes Boykin-Sabalones is B2B specialist SE (co-sell). Strong internal conviction — Nathaniel has already done the internal selling.

competition.text: Status quo (NetSuite SuiteCommerce) is the primary competition. No other platforms mentioned. NetSuite engineers themselves acknowledged SuiteCommerce was not designed for B2B — strong position for Shopify B2B.

viaggio-pontoons.strategy: Co-sell with Joe Hemingway (AE). Mercedes is B2B specialist SE. Champion Nathaniel built the internal business case himself — high internal conviction. Decision deadline end of June 2026. Critical path: receive requirements doc (May 22-23) → technical discovery May 28-29 → proposal before June 30. Multi-brand expansion (Lakota, SMC, Duo Form) is the long-term prize. Must get Mercedes + Joe in front of Owner Eric and CFO before June 30.

risks.text: Decision deadline end of June 2026 — very compressed timeline for complex B2B + D2C platform architecture with multi-brand scope
risks.mit: Move immediately: review requirements doc May 22-23, nail technical discovery May 28-29, proposal by early June.

risks.text: Economic buyer (Owner Eric, CFO) not yet in direct contact — Nathaniel is only intermediary. Deal could stall if Eric is not fully committed.
risks.mit: Joe + Mercedes to get on a call with Eric and CFO before June close. Nathaniel's internal pitch is strong but needs validation from Mercedes/Joe directly.

risks.text: Dealer configurator with rule-based pricing logic — complexity not yet documented (8-page requirements doc arriving May 22-23)
risks.mit: Review requirements doc closely before May 28-29 technical discovery. Flag configurator as key scoping topic.

risks.text: Co-sell complexity — Joe is AE, Mercedes is B2B specialist SE, dedicated SE for technical discovery not yet confirmed
risks.mit: Align with Joe on roles and division of labor before May 28-29 call.

viaggio-pontoons.notes: Co-sell: Joe Hemingway (AE, joseph.hemingway@shopify.com), Mercedes Boykin-Sabalones (B2B specialist SE). SF opp 006OG00000NP5QgYAL owned by Joe. Multi-brand marine manufacturer: Viaggio Pontoons, Lakota Trailers, SMC, Duo Form (Bristol, Indiana). Nathaniel Talbot (Director of IT, ntalbot@viaggiopontoons.com / ntalbot@lakotatrailers.com) is champion — built 5-yr cost analysis internally. 137 dealers, 5 territories, 7 sales reps. DTC very limited; apparel outsourced. Shipping restrictions (e.g. can't ship 4-boat load to WA state). Decision deadline: end of June 2026.

manager_q.compelling_event: Decision deadline end of June 2026 — Nathaniel: 'if we don't have a decision by end of June, we're going to be scrambling'
